pageTitle: Username Generator - Random Word Combination Generator Free | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Instantly generate a random username or email name idea by combining two adjectives and a noun. Choose your separator, casing style, and optional number suffix — free, no sign-up.
softwareAppDescription: A free web tool that instantly generates a random username by combining two adjectives and a noun, using your browser's cryptographically secure random number generator (crypto.getRandomValues).
subtitle: Combine random words to get a fresh username or email name idea
taglineExtra: Adjust the separator, casing, and number suffix to fit exactly what you need.
loadError: Couldn't load the word list. Please reload the page.
infoIntroP: Username Generator instantly creates a fresh, random name by combining two adjectives and a noun — useful when you're stuck picking a name for a new email address, a game handle, a project codename, or any account that needs a unique identifier. It draws from a curated bank of hundreds of adjectives and nouns and uses your browser's cryptographically secure random number generator (crypto.getRandomValues), so every combination is genuinely unpredictable rather than following an obvious pattern.
infoHowtoP: Click "Generate new" to get a random combination right away. Use the separator dropdown to join the words with a dot, underscore, hyphen, or nothing at all, and pick a letter case style (lowercase, camelCase, or PascalCase) to match the platform you're signing up for. Turn on "Add number" if you want a random number suffix — handy since plain word combinations are often already taken. Once you like the result, click "Copy" to copy it to your clipboard.
infoUseCases: Picking a name for a new email address
Coming up with a username for a game or online community
Naming a new project, repository, or codename
Getting inspiration when your first-choice username is already taken

faq.a: Each result combines two different adjectives and one noun, drawn randomly from a curated word bank of several hundred entries each.

faq.q: Is the result sent to a server?
faq.a: No. Every combination is generated entirely in your browser using the Web Crypto API — nothing is sent to or stored on a server.

faq.q: Can I get a username without numbers?
faq.a: Yes. Turn off the "Add number" checkbox and the result will only contain the three words, joined with whatever separator you chose.

faq.q: Why use crypto.getRandomValues instead of Math.random?
faq.a: Math.random() is a predictable pseudo-random generator, not intended for cases where genuine unpredictability matters. crypto.getRandomValues() is the same cryptographically secure random source browsers use for security-sensitive operations, so combinations don't follow a guessable pattern.

privacyNote: This tool generates every username entirely in your browser. Nothing you generate is ever sent to or stored on a server.
